5 stars!“Some battles are best fought with a sidekick.”I seriously cannot stop reading/listening to these books. In less than a week, I’ve listened to 1, 1.5, and 2 (which is over 40 hours of audio) I’m not reading much at all because I am incapable of pushing pause on this all-consuming series! Now I keep telling myself to slow down because what will I listen to next when this series is over?!? I am loving these books so much, and this is my favorite of the series so far. It still focuses on my favorite couple, Lily and Lo, but we also get more of the other couples. There is love, laughter, healing, and even a little suspense!“I need you to know that I’m not going anywhere. I’ll always be here when you come home. There will always be an us.”Someone has told the world about Lily Calloway’s sex addiction. This destroys Lily. Lo is newly sober, but is determined to be there for Lily no matter what. He is her rock. She needs him to help with her addiction and to be there to love and support her, and he does 100%.The couple is on spring break vacation with the rest of the gang for a good part of the book. It brings new challenges for Lily and Lo and gives you more time with the other characters as well. The best part about this one…dual pov! That’s right my friends- we get Lily AND Lo’s perspective. It was great being in both of their heads and I think it added so much more to the story.Lily and Lo are two characters I love so much. Everyone talks about how much they love Connor and Ryke in these books, and they’re great, but for me, Lo is the favorite. I love how much he loves Lily. How understanding he is, how he never judges her. He just loves her. He sees her more than a sex addict. She is worth everything to him.She shrunk into herself and waited for me to stare at her like she was gross and dirty. But I just wanted to hold her and tell her that she was worth so much more than whatever she was searching for.It makes me so happy to see this couple overcome some of their obstacles. They are characters you will root for. The writing in these books are flawless. I am enjoying this series so much.Addicted for Nowgets a full 5 stars from me. I can’t wait to get into the next one, which is the first spin off and features Connor and Rose. Even though Rose isn’t my favorite character, I have a feeling it won’t disappoint!

♾ stars“I’m not going anywhere. I’ll always be here when you come home. There will always be an us.”“A Lo and Lily,” I breathe.“Or Lily and Lo.”I want to inject this story, these words, into my veins, into my blood and soul, so I can never get Lily and Loren out of my system. This was the book that made me fall in love with Lilo and their breathtaking and soulmate-like love story, and this reread just proved how much I love them and how precious Core Six is to me. This book taught me a lot—this series and these characters basically altered my brain chemistry, and I will never be the same.Addictions are one of the most horrible things in the world. They not only destroy the addict but also those who are exposed to their lives. And seeing it all—the messiness, the destruction, the willingness to do right by the people you love, the slow healing, the healthy coping mechanisms, the second chances, it’s the most realistic portrayal I have read in a series.Lily’sportrayal in this book as a struggling sex addict who is in therapy, learning to have a healthy sex life while also working on healthy coping mechanisms, is remarkably done. Her healing is a two-way process, she’s trying to show trust in others by letting them help her with her addictions. And Lo did so much for her in this book, along with her sister, Rose. She still feels like she doesn’t deserve happiness or a normal life, or that being a sex addict is something dirty that needs to be hidden from everyone. She’s trying her best to accept her new self—the version that loves Loren, the one who’s trying so hard to do her best for herself, achieving new milestones in her healing, and I am so proud of my girl.“He is as much a part of me as the sun is a part of the sky, as the earth is to the universe. I need him in order to wake up in the morning. I need him to feel whole.”Loren Halehas a permanent place in my heart. I have never loved a fictional character this much, never felt so connected to one like I do with him. Lo is a recovering alcoholic in this book. After coming back from rehab, he’s trying to make sense of his reality as a non-alcoholic while also helping Lily with her addiction. He still struggles every day, hates himself, but he makes an effort not to relapse. Lily and Ryke are his biggest support system, the people who make him stronger and happier. Lo still doesn’t see himself as a good person. He hates and blames himself for the life Lily and Ryke have lived in the past, but he’s wrong about it all. He is not responsible for anyone’s happiness. This man has infinite love for Lily but not even a drop of it for himself, but I know he’ll get there one day, and I am here for him.“She loves me because she believes I’m strong. She loves me because she’s a part of me. She loves me because I’ve become a better man through all of this.”Lily and LorenBest friends. Lovers. Soulmates.I love these two amazing and incredible people with every fibre of my being. To put it simply, I come alive whenever I read their story or even just think about them. They have made a home for themselves in my life and my heart. I think about them daily—they are a part of my life, literally. I don’t think it’s possible to find a love like theirs in this lifetime because Lilo is the true and raw definition of soulmates. It was pure pain and agony to watch Lilo struggle to find a semblance of balance in their lives while also battling their addictions, sustaining their present life and friendships, and dealing with the repercussions of their past. But they came out of it alive and together, and that’s all that matters. They didn’t give up on themselves—not on each other—and they had friends supporting them throughout their journey.“In all our life, he has never once said to me, you are mine. He has never taken me the way I’ve taken him. He has given himself to me.”The couple’s growth is beautifully done. Lily and Loren are finding a new rhythm in their intimate yet still raw relationship, which could crumble at any moment. But they are putting in the effort, truly opening up to each other about their struggles and the hardships that come with their recovery process. They chose effort over sacrifice, took a chance on a future they don’t even dare to dream of yet, but one that exists for them. They took a chance to help and understand each other—to let trust, love, and healing become the most integral parts of their lives. I broke for them many times, but every time they fought back, my heart swelled with so much pride and love for them. I believed in them the first time I read their books, and I will believe in them for years to come.“We have lived together, loved together, and fucked up together. I’m not sure many people can truly say that about someone else.”All the side characters were incredible, and seeing them help Lilo was reassuring and comforting. I love how protective Rose and Connor are—I loved seeing them fall in love. Ryke didn’t get on my nerves here because he is unwavering and loving when it comes to Lo, and I love him for that. I love Daisy and Ryke, but I still can’t forget Ryke’s shameless flirting with Daisy, knowing she’s just a teenager. It gives me the ick.“I think the six of us—we’re all strong. We’re each just a different kind of strong. But we all have a different kind of weak too.”Addicted for Nowis the most heartbreaking book in the Addicted series. My heart was breaking on every page, but it was all worth it in the end. This is where the real character development of Lily and Loren begins. They are still the same people but have shown immense growth. They cannot just be magically healed in one book, and there is a long journey ahead of them, but at least they are fighting for their future. They are finally making changes in their previously dysfunctional relationship to make it work because their love is bigger than their addictions. This book deals with heavy topics, and I loved how the authors did not try to sugarcoat their addictions but actually represented the dark and broken side of recovery.“I’m prepared to be there for her every fucking step of the way. Even if she wants to drown in this water, I’m going to pull her back up until she’s healthy. Until she can stand on her own two feet.”The final chapters are a rollercoaster of raw and vulnerable emotions. Loren doubting his own existence, Lily going through that traumatic and disgusting experience with her new therapist—it broke my heart. But the last chapter was everything. Loren Hale, how many times are you going to steal my heart? The proposal was so perfect and romantic, and I am so happy and excited to see how they are going to thrive and grow in the next phases of their lives.“She is my past, my present, my future. So when I open the door and slide into the driver’s seat, I’m not surprised that it feels like I’m returning home.”➙ Second read: June 2024 — ♾ stars➙ First read: September 2022 — 5 stars—————I don't know what to do with my life rn 🥺 someone pls inject this epic love story in my veins so I can never get lilo and core six out of my system. Rtc!—————Rereading bcs i missed my family and bcs i am in a really bad slump and nothing is working for me and in core six we trust 😌

FIRST FIVE STARS FOR THE ADDICTED SERIES!OBSESSED OBSESSED OBSESSED OBSESSEDOVERALL THOUGHTSYou guys have NO IDEA how much I've grown to love this series. I feel like I personally know the characters. I'M ONE OF THE GANG! I love it so much already SUBJECTIVELY, but I also must say that it got much better OBJECTIVELY. It's so visible that the authors are living, breathing and talking through these characters. They understand the characters and their actions so well, and no matter how easy to achieve that sounds, trust me it's NOT. The „of course they know their own characters“ doesn't really work in fiction, especially in contemporary romance. And also, the first book was objectively pretty bad (I really liked the story don't hate me) but the execution wasn't good. The characters were 2dimensional and basic. And to come from such a wattpady way of writing to something this good doesn't deserve anything short of 5 stars. Also, I can confidently say this since I've changed my way of reading (this last book I listened to as an audiobook) and it was equally good. Yes, changing the method of reading can sometimes affect my judgment (or so I think).I'm so thankful to the girlies on here who WOULDN'T STOP RAVING about this series, I'M SO SERIOUS THANK YOU, I'M having the time of my life. 💖💖💖💖💖💖A PSYCHOTIC BREAK-THROUGH THE CHARACTERSLILY – My baby, I would give this girl the world. Yes, she's still very immature and messy, but let's be fair, her life was just like that and let the girl HEAL. And also, for every bad thing she has done, she PAID two times more. I truly enjoyed her POVs in this one and can't wait to talk to her again.LO- I'm amazed at how much he has grown as a character. HE'S THE GUY YOU SHOULD BE OBSESSING OVER (just so you know I'm so fr about this one). My man is committed to being sober and now, I feel like we really got to see how kind he's at heart. Also, I just lovelovelove how he became a businessman (not going to spoil this any further), I'm so excited to see where's this portion of the story going to go.RYKE- HEAR ME OUT,,,,,,PlsPlsI hated this guy in the first two books (and I sort of still do hate him for the situation with Daisy) BUT I might give him a second chance. He was much better in this book, really sassy also, I like his game, I trully find him so amusing. We're going to see (really soon) if I'll regret this.DAISY- my spirit animal, my sunshine. I love her so much, and out of all the characters, I honestly relate to her the most. Maybe it's based on the lack of knowledge on her character, BUT, she's my comfort person. I can't wait to see how is her story going to expand (more her independently than the romance part tbh). This girl is so full of life, I just have so much love for her.ROSE- WELL, my least favorite Calloway sister so far, maybe based (again) on the lack of knowledge. I have no information on her whatsoever, besides her being EXTREMELY AND UNNECESSARY PRUDE. But she can stand up for herself and the people she loves, and that's strong and beautiful (I LOVE IT WHEN WOMEN)CONNOR- I CAN'T EMPHASIZE THIS ENOUGH, SOMETHING IS UP WITH THIS GUY, MY SCORPIO MOON KNOWS THAT SOMETHING'S NOT RIGHT. I would legit skip the next book (I know it's ROSE&CONNOR) if it weren't for my curiosity (does it kill the cat??) I don't particularly like him as a character, I JUST WANNA KNOW WHAT IS HE HIDING.Immediately starting the next bookHope you enjoyed whatever this was lol 😆

“I need you to know that I’m not going anywhere. I’ll always be here when you come home. There will always be an us. “A Lo and Lily,” I breathe. “Or Lily and Lo.”I think this might be my favorite book of the series even without having finished the others. It was genuinelyphenomenaland I’m so so happy I devoured it as slowly as I could.After both books were in Lily’s pov and Ricochet focused on her growth and recovery with her addiction — it meant the world to me to finally hear lo’s pov to see what was in that complex mind of his as it was completely essential to understanding him and his struggles with his addictionAddicted for now encompasses both lily and lo’s continued journey towards their recovery. They’re both still fighting the hollowness and urges that come with their addictions, still receiving the utmost beautiful support they could have and more, and still at odds with how to navigate through it all — but ultimately both are there for one another in ways their addictions didn’t allow them to be before.As things seem to be progressing in their recovery, something unexpected happens that threatens to hinder the progress they’ve made from rehab and therapy. The threatening aspect gave the book a lil suspense/mystery to it and I was all for it. It was such a shocker to say the least. Anyway, here you could say lily and lo are both done enabling one another and it’s up to them to fight for themselves than to sink back into old habits.I knew to expect the highs and lows that comes with recovery and that is definitely what you’ll get here. It was refreshing to see all of it portrayed so brutally honest, realistically, flawlessly, heartwarming, humorously at times, and addictively I should say. I loved and didn’t want it to end.The love here between lilo felt so safe, handled with such care and the way lo handled all that came with lily's addiction (while also struggling himself) and at the guise of her therapist?? I cherished it. I truly adored lo so much in this I couldn’t get enough of him. It’s hard to romanticize a character whose dealing and in recovery from an addiction but that aside—lo and his heart when it comes to lily and how he supports and speaks to her had me swooning.Their relationship and the love for one another was also way less toxic and much healthier and it just managed to surpass their initial enabling and destructive behaviors as both are evidently each others anchors. They trulydeserveall the best 🥹I could go on for days of all that I loved about them, but it wouldn’t do it justice as reading it firsthand would.I was so much more consumed here with the little found family lo and lily found with her sisters and Connor and Ryke — all differences aside. I enjoyed seeing all of them working together to help them both and treasured it very much and this solidified my appreciation and love for the found family trope with ALL of themI’m definitely looking forward to Connors and rose’s book bc of their obvious banter, their conversations, and being academic rivals just left me intrigued for moreKnowing about what transpires between Ryke and Daisy was inevitable. It’s difficult not to see the obvious difference between them being inappropriate. But I’d be lying if I said I wasn’t curious to know how it’ll be explored in their book.As this was dual pov, it was so engaging to read through. The Ritchie sisters managed to write and pace everything so smoothly and beautifully and it has undeniably become one of my favorite series and lilo my favorite flawed humans.Some parts quotes“And I’m going to move so slow that three months ago will feel like yesterday. And tomorrow will feel like today, and no one in this fucking universe will be able to say your name without saying mine.”“He is my breath. My soul. My life-force … He’s Loren Hale. Ice and whiskey. Powerful and intoxicating. He’s my very best friend.”"We're supposed to be getting better." “And there will be roadblocks and setbacks," "just because you hit one doesn't mean you let me down. If anything, I'm proud of you for handling it like this.""Lo," she says, "you've made it. You're my Scott Summers, and without you, I wouldn't be here."“I’ll show you.” 🫠 literally screamed here (page 507 I think)“I'm ready to start the newest chapter of our lives. One where we're not assaulted by our vices. One where we're happy.”“A female sex addict? A slut. A male sex addict? A hero.”“You just have to believe that you’ll be strong enough in the end. Even if the middle is all fucked up.”“Being sober isn’t our only challenge. Becoming human, functioning people in this big vast world means making friendships and sustaining the very few that we already have.”“I think the six of us—we’re all strong. We’re each just a different kind of strong. But we all have a different kind of weak too.”

i am so happy i pushed through this book because i really ended up loving it! the first 2 books and the first half of this book just weren’t really doing it for me, i felt like it was a lot of ‘world building’ (us getting to know characters, dynamics, and situations that are important to the story) but once this book started rolling IT STARTED ROLLING. there was so much drama to keep me entertained and i LOVE all of these characters in their own ways! i cant wait to see what these stories have to hold! i’m going to be so sad when it’s all over

not me forgetting to write a review to this book, forgive me plz. in my defense.. I was BAWLING my eyes out, I couldn’t even see straight so please picture me trying to write my feelings. anyways. (updating as looking back this is probably more like a 4 star read rather than 5)My love for this series continues to grow with every book, and I’m so thankful the journey isn’t over yet 🫶🏻 I love the characters. I love the story. I love the friendships and relationships. I seriously love everything about it.With the previous books being mostly about Lily’s addiction and recovery, we finally get to see how Lo is coping with his own. I’ve never wanted a dual POV more, I was so happy when I saw the chapter names and realised we were finally getting it!!! And let me tell you, it does not disappoint. He struggles through not only with his own battles, but with Lily’s too. The dedication and devotion he has for her makes me so happy to see and I love him so much more for it.“Hey, Lil…” His thumb skims my cheek. “You’re okay.” He must recognize the fear in my eyes. “I won’t ever desert you, love. Not for a goddamn moment. You’re mine to take care of, you understand?”Lily and Lo have to learn how to handle their relationship in new ways which is more difficult than ever before. There’s some mystery mixed in when the unthinkable happens, and the things they both have to deal with is honestly so heartbreaking.I am so proud of the way they handle everything that’s thrown at them. They deal with all that whilst also trying their best to evolve their relationship into something healthier. They’re there for each other whenever things get hard no matter what. Gone is the toxicity and the enabling. Their journey is written with such raw emotion that it’s impossible not to feel every high and low along with them. I just want the best for them 🥺💘There’s some scenes near the end of the book between Lily and her new psychiatrist which were very uncomfortable, but the portrayal of it was done really well and just shows the honest and stark reality of what it’s like for some going through therapy.It’s not all heartbreak though and there are a ton of sweet moments throughout the book. One of my favourites being the idea Lo comes up with to provide for himself. It’s so perfect and kinda like a full circle moment 🦸🏻🗯️I think the six of us—we’re all strong. We’re each just a different kind of strong. But we all have a different kind of weak too. And I’m figuring out how to bottle my weakness to help them all.Lily and Lo continue to have support throughout the entire journey from Rose, Connor, Daisy, and Ryke. I love this little found family and I enjoyed all their moments together. They each have their own stories going on, which I can’t wait to read more about next in the Calloway Sisters series 🤭

“If the whole world says living without each other is what we should do, then this will be the last wrong I make.”I love LilyLo with my whole goddamn heart and I will not hear a single bad word about them because I feel them so deeply 😭 no one could ever make me hate you!Loren Haleis the definition of devoted. The depth of his dedication and protection for Lil, whilst simultaneously battling his own addiction and recovery - I’ll cry actually 🥺It is the way he never ever wavered from Lily, he never stopped supporting her, loving her and putting her above all else. Knowing how much it hurt him at times but he did what was best for her, my heart aches! His growth and development is tough yet heartwarming all in one & the fact his sole motivation is Lily… and I will cry.This man is SO hot - sorry, not even remotely sorry. Oh my god.“Eyes on me, love,”- standing ovulation 🙂‍↕️Lily Calloway, my beautiful girl. I just adore her. Her character development was on point. The way she loves Lo, I can feel it so deeply in my own soul. My sweet girl is battling so much, not just her addiction but also her mental health struggles. I just know she put absolutely everything she had into wanting to be better for herself but mostly for her and Lo. She is just a girl who is hurt, confused, struggling and is scared. I just want to sweep her up into a big hug and tell her that I’ve got her back no matter what.”Only Lo can satisfy every part of my all-consuming soul. He is truly my everything.”🥹Their dynamic is something really special. I just adored how intertwined they are with each other, and that they never lost that through their navigation to a new healthly normal together. They both worked so hard to get on the path to recovery but ultimately neither of them did it alone and without each other they wouldn’t be healing, I truly believe this highlights that they are one… it doesn’t take away from their individual efforts but it’s just “A Lo and Lil” 🥹 they learnt how to still be each other orbit whilst letting others in. I’m so proud of my them.There is something about childhood best friends to lovers that hits so hard, when you know their love has been there from the get go. Knowing all these years they could have been together!! Please, nothing makes sense without them together, if it's not LiLo then I don't want it!”He is as much a part of me as the sun is a part of the sky, as the Earth is to the universe.”The plot was stressing me out here, aside from LiLo's developments - the conflict had my anxiety through the roof!!The core 6 are on track to be one of my new favourite found families.Seeing Rose and Connor continuously do all they could for Lily, in the most judgement free and safe way - I just adore them so much. I cannot wait to get into their books because I am already ridiculously obsessed with them!!! I just know their books are going to have me in a chokehold, because I can already deeply relate to Rose's character and how Connor is so patient with her. I said it before and I will say it again, I am not ready for this man!!Ryke and Lo's relationship development shined for me throughout this one too. I love how fiercely Ryke stands by Lo and wants him to be happy and thriving. They both were hurt by those who were meant to love them unconditionally but out of that, their relationship thrived and became something beautiful.Side note, the Mum is a massive bitch, idgaf, she is awful. The amount of times I wanted to climb into the book and slap this hag around the head. Do not pmo like this, please. I hate her, alongside every other piece of garbage in this book. Everyone that wronged my babies need to line up and get behind me LiLo… I got you!!! 🤸🏼‍♂️🤺

not me rating EVERY book so far in this series 5 stars.this was the third book in the series (i’m following the recommended reading order) and i simply got everything i could have asked for from it. but this time not only was there struggle, love, friendship, family, and everything else that comes with this series…there was also a touch of mystery. and if you know me you know i’m a mystery girlie so i ate that uppp. i also found myself loving so many quotes and smiling.lo and lily are learning how to navigate their new way of living, and with that comes painful moments but also great ones that had me clapping for them. but something is making overcoming their addictions much harder for them: someone is threatening to tell the world about lily’s addiction. who could it possibly be? what will lily do? ryke, connor, rose, and lo stay by her side as they try to figure this out. but even in the drama, there’s lighthearted moments and new characters to meet, great therapy representation, and an ending that has me internally SCREAMING.*Trigger warnings for alcoholism, sex addiction, sexually explicit scenes, mention of/attempt at sexual assault*

★★★★★5+ 'I'm addicted to this series' stars!I absolutely love everything about this series. It's brutally honest, realistic, easy to read, funny, heartbreaking and heartwarming all at the same time. Lo and Lily, the two main characters, are easily one of my favorite fictional couples ever. As we already knew from the previous two books in the series, they each have their own addiction. He's addicted to alcohol, she's addicted to sex. This time the story is told from alternating POV's and I loved the fact that we finally get Lo's side of the story as well.****From here on out there might beSPOILERSfor those who haven't read the previous books yet****Most of their adult life they've been enabling each others addiction by covering up for the other one and by pretending that everything was fine all the while knowing that what they were doing was far from okay. Now, they've finally faced their problems. Lo is coming home from rehab and together they will try to stand their ground, to not let their addiction win and to built up a healthy, addiction-free and happy future."I'm sick too," he says, " and there will be times where we're weak. Where we beg for the things we can't have. But you can't be scared of that, Lil. You just have to believe that you'll be strong enough in the end. Even if the middle is all fucked up.""He is my breath. My soul. My life-force. I have spent forever with him. Being apart is the most unnatural feeling in the world."Being away from Lo has not been easy for Lily. She's been able to stay celibate during the three months he spent in rehab but now that they're moving in together, it's becoming harder and harder for her to avoid the temptations. They sleep in the same bed every night but unlike before, when they were still guided by their addictions, they can't have sex every night. Lily's new psychiatrist has given them a list of do's and don't and time limits they need to keep. All of this ways hard on both Lily and Lo. While she struggles with being denied sex, he struggles with having to tell her no all the time."Lily doens't do hugs. She fucks until she passes out. And I so badly want to fix her, but I can only help her. The real mending-that has to be her job, her fight, her battle. I can't win this one for her - just like she can't defeat my demons."At the same time, Lo still struggles with his own demons. And on top of all that someone has started threatening him to expose Lily's secret to the whole wide world. If that were ever to happen, it would destroy not only her family's reputation, but the one thing she desperately counts on, her privacy. Therefore Lo has taken it upon himself to do whatever it takes to avoid her secret from coming out."I won't ever desert you, love. Not for a goddamn moment. You're mine to take care of, you understand?"Throughout all of this Lily and Lo have the full support of Lily's sisters Rose and Daisy, Lo's half brother Ryke and Rose's boyfriend Connor. The four of them are some of the few people Lo and Lily can count on and together the six of them have become very close friends. I loved these side characters and their own interesting stories and personalities almost, but not quite, as much as the main couple itself and I'm already looking forward to their own spin-offs!This is not your average NA romance series. This series tackles some very realistic and uncommon subjects and paints them in a very refreshing, interesting light. It's main focus is on two people who are each struggling to overcome their own personal addiction by being there for each other and helping the other one overcome their demons. It's a unique and powerful series and I was really happy with the way this second book ended (no cliffhanger). The third book can't be here fast enough though, I already want more Lo and Lily!! ;)"There will always be an us.""A Lo and Lily.""Or Lily and Lo."

➳ 5 ☆“i depend on him more than any girl should depend on a boy, but he’s been the backbone of my life. without him, i will fall.”besties... i think i fucked up again, because i in-fact, found a new series that has become my obsession. i need to stop doing this for the sake of my mental health.➺ lily calloway~ this entire plot/story revolves around her character. i do really like her, however, i think of the core six, i relate to her the least. i found her growth to be very realistic and salient, and i really love her relationship with connor and ryke. i know people find her annoying because of the whole "quirky" girl persona, but that didn't really disconcert me. she is a little self centered and that kind of annoys me. i hope she starts making better decisions moving forward.➺ loren hale~ i really liked him?? i know, i am shocked too. i found him to be quite irksome in the initial books but his determination and loyalty got me. there isn't too much to say about him since a lot of this novel was about lily, and how he was there for her. he truly grew a backbone and developed brain cells in this. i fear i have to stan.“i deserve every cold look, every biting comment. i deserve that fucking pain.”“and there will be roadblocks and setbacks,” he tells me, “just because you hit one doesn’t mean you let me down. if anything, i’m proud of you for handling it like this.”“i let out a long breath, bearing her pain that feels so much a part of me. we’ve become entangled, years and years of lies and childhood memories and stories all wrapped into one. i know her better than her sisters. i know her sometimes better than she does herself.”“my twenty-two-year-old brother is flirting, apparently not deliberately—i’m not even sure how that fucking happens—with your sixteen-year-old sister.”➺ rose calloway~ i called that she would be my favorite and i know i was right. she again, does not have much of a presence in these books just yet, but her book is next!!! you already know i am so ready for that.➺ (richard) connor cobalt~ i think i like men who mind their own business and don't speak, because guess what? he's also my favorite. connor was the only person in this book whose deportment matched their age. i am so ready to get to know him better in "kiss the sky".connor raises his hands. “i didn’t say a word.” her eyes soften at him. “you’re exempt.”she possesses him, but he’s equally as possessive of her.➺ daisy calloway~ well.... her character still made me excruciatingly uncomfortable. she shows up toward the halfway point and she pretty much leaves after a certain section of the book. however, when she did show up..... yikes. her sunshine persona is definitely a cover, and i am intrigued to see her real personality and struggles come through with her books.“daisy had cash in her bikini top,” lo says. “ryke made some offhanded comment about it and it led to… other comments.”➺ (jonathan) ryke *i am still scared of him* meadows~ i like that he is loyal and caring toward his friends/family, however, his relationship with daisy makes me so uneasy. very similar to daisy, i know the whole moody, broody, grumpy bullshit is a facade, so i am excited to see him develop and become his own character. this whole thing could've been avoided had they just made daisy eighteen or nineteen.... the comments were so odd, i am sorry. other than that, he is growing on me, and i feel like i could like his character more than loren (?).opinions of the three core couples ↴➼ lilo~ they are your typical best friends to lovers meets the fake dating trope. i really do love that their relationship is filled with mundane moments where it's just them going through their day to day lives. they both are also very protective of each other and ardent they are about the things they love and care about. sadly, i do think they rank at the bottom of the three couples.➼ cobolloway~ i think i've made it clear that they both are my favorite. i just love rivals to lovers, especially with a "mean girl" and a quiet smart man obsessed simp. i am so ready for their book.➼ raisy~ i don't hate them, but their dynamic makes me uncomfortable. i do like that they seem to understand each other and are the adrenaline junkies of the group. everyone says "hothouse flower" is the best book, and i am really looking forward to that. i hope they make no references to her younger days, and their book is finding home in each other since they tend to be the ones taking care of others. i also want this book to be filled with tension and angst. i think that could win me over since ryke seems possessive and overtop when it comes to daisy.overall, this series has a VERY slow start which is why it took me years to get back into this world. i don't really know how i feel about ten books—especially having the last ones being so long—following six characters. i do really like the idea of a long series that is character driven, so i hope this doesn't start to lag in the latter half of the series.
